candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above finance section and outside the selected COGS row 'Raw materials and consumables', so it does not appear embedded in the selected COGS bucket. However, it is generic and not identified as COGS-specific.

Separate operating D&A line above finance section and outside the selected COGS row 'Raw materials and consumables', so it does not appear embedded in the selected COGS bucket. However, it is generic and not identified as COGS-specific.

variables.notes: The statement shows generic operating D&A lines at the same level as the selected operating expense rows, but nothing indicates what portion belongs specifically to COGS versus SG&A. To avoid arbitrary allocation and double counting, no COGS-specific D&A row is selected.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above finance section and outside the selected XSGA rows 'Cost of personnel' and 'Other operating expenses', so it does not appear embedded in the selected XSGA bucket. But the label is generic and may include non-SG&A amounts, including production-related depreciation.

Separate operating D&A line above finance section and outside the selected XSGA rows 'Cost of personnel' and 'Other operating expenses', so it does not appear embedded in the selected XSGA bucket. But the label is generic and may include non-SG&A amounts.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket explicitly excluded D&A, and generic operating depreciation/amortisation lines are shown separately. However, the excerpt does not identify whether these amounts belong to SG&A, COGS, or mixed operating functions. Because R&D is only mapped from a supplemental line below operating profit and there is no explicit operating 'Amortization in R&D' row here, the conservative choice is to leave XSGA_DA empty pending manual review.
